items.final_wording: Supports documenting an abnormal hirsutism score, progression, menstrual disturbance, other hyperandrogenic features, family or ethnic context and clinician-selected androgen or 17-hydroxyprogesterone testing.

source.title: Evaluation and Treatment of Hirsutism in Premenopausal Women: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline
source.jurisdiction: International specialty guideline; UAE laboratory, prescribing and referral policy governs local use
source.population: Premenopausal women with patient-important hirsutism or an abnormal hirsutism score.
source.setting: Clinician evaluation and longitudinal review of hirsutism in endocrine, primary-care or reproductive-health services.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting progression, menstrual or hyperandrogenic context, patient-important impact, clinician-selected androgen testing and clinician-discussed review. It does not establish a cause or authorise treatment.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting an abnormal hirsutism score, progression, menstrual disturbance, other hyperandrogenic features, family or ethnic context and clinician-selected androgen or 17-hydroxyprogesterone testing.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting an abnormal hirsutism score, progression, menstrual disturbance, other hyperandrogenic features, family or ethnic context and clinician-selected androgen or 17-hydroxyprogesterone testing.
